original: Multicellular organisms (bdelloid rotifer) frozen for 24,000 years in Siberia to return to life after Russian scientists have them thawed
New study reveals Shark Bay sea grass, off Western Australia, may be world's largest plant, covering 77 square miles of sea floor, having cloned itself for 4,500 years
Mars Curiosity Rover finds organic matter, including methane, on Mars in studies published in journal "Science"
Earliest-ever evidence of Homo Sapiens from Jebel Irhoud, Morocco unearthed by archaeologists published in "Nature", at 300,000 years old
Edward "Bear" Grylls, British adventurer, author and TV presenter (Man vs. Wild), born in Donaghadee, Northern Ireland
George Leigh-Mallorydisappears 775' from Everest's summit
1st verifiable ascent of main summit of Denali (Mt McKinley), North America's highest mountain led by Hudson Stuck and Harry Karstens
Mount Pinatubo (Philippines) erupts for 1st time
Gandhiis thrown off a First Class compartment of a Pretoria train in Pietermaritzburg, South Africa - leads to his first act of civil disobedience when he protests his treatment the next day
Graceland,Elvis Presley's home, is opened to the public
Black Lives Matter Protests continue worldwide in large numbers, In Bristol England statue of 17th century slave trader Edward Colston pulled down
"Harry Potter and the Cursed Child", a play written by Jack Thorne with JK Rowling, premieres in London
16th century archaeology remains of the Curtain Theatre, where some ofShakespeare's plays first performed found under a pub in London
New York's air quality sinks to the lowest in the world (air quality index 218) as smoke from Canadian wildfires blankets the American north east
Britain's Boxford Timber identified as UK's oldest decoratively carved wood at 6,000 years old by radio carbon dating after being found in West Berkshire during house renovations
Earliest report of ball lightning in London by Benedictine monk Gervase of Christ Church Cathedral Priory, Canterbury
NASA civilian test pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 31,580 m
Soviet Soyuz 11 crew completes 1st transfer to orbiting Salyut space station
Battle of Midway ends: AdmiralChester Nimitzwins 1st World War II naval defeat of Japan
David Filoni, American director, producer, and screenwriter (Star Wars: The Clone Wars (2008-20 TV series); The Mandalorian), born in Mt. Lebanon, Pennsylvania
American singer Prince celebrates his birthday by changing his name to an unpronounceable symbol
For one second this morning, the time is 01:23:45, 6-7-89
The Beatles travel canals of Amsterdam, with a substitute for Ringo
James Jude, American thoracic surgeon (developed CPR), born in Maple Lake, Minnesota (d. 2015)
Virginia Apgar, American physician and childbirth specialist, born in Westfield, New Jersey (d. 1974)
US army tests 1st machine gun mounted on a plane
Vatnajökull National Park in Iceland formed as second-largest European national park, combining Skaftafell and Jökulsárgljúfur parks and the Vatnajökull ice cap (World Heritage Site 2019)
American songwriter and impresarioBerry GordyJr. founds Tamla Records, precursor to Motown Records, in Detroit, Michigan
An earthquake in Port Royal, Jamaica, kills 3,000 and sinks the cemetery whereHenry Morganwas buried into the sea
14-year-old Mughal princeAurangzeb, the future Emperor Alamgir I, survives an elephant attack
First baseball game to start outdoors and end indoors as Toronto Blue Jays' stadium closes roof during game at 8:48 and beat Brewers 4-2

Allen Iverson, American Basketball HOF guard (NBA Rookie of the Year Award 1997; 11 x NBA All-Star; All-Star game MVP 2001, 05; NBA MVP 2001; 3 × All-NBA First Team; Philadelphia 76ers), born in Hampton, Virginia
Bill Hader, American comedian and actor (Saturday Night Live, Barry), born in Tulsa, Oklahoma
Wayne Gretzkywins his 9th NHL Hart (MVP) Trophy in 10 years
MLB Oakland A's outfielderRickey Hendersonsteals his 1,100th career base
MLB Draft: Southern Nevada outfielderBryce Harperfirst pick by Washington Nationals
Mike Modano, American Hockey Hall of Fame center, 1989-2011, 8X All-Star (Minnesota North Stars/Dallas Stars, Detroit Red Wings), born in Livonia, Michigan
Quannah Chasinghorse, Native American model, born in Tuba City, Arizona
Anne McClain, American army officer and NASA astronaut (Soyuz MS-11), born in Spokane, Washington
Cafu, Brazilian soccer defender (Brazil 142 caps, Roma, AC Milan), born in Itaquaquecetuba, Brazil
Louise Erdrich, American author (Love Medicine, The Night Watchman, 2021 Pulitzer Prize), born in Little Falls, Minnesota
Bob DylanandJohnny Cashperform duet on a "Grand Ole Opry" TV special
British rock band The Who's perform their rock opera "Tommy" at the Metropolitan Opera House at NYC's Lincoln Center
Boeing 314 Clipper flying boat 1st flown (Eddie Allen)
Melvin Jones and a number of other Chicago businessmen found Lions Clubs International, now the largest service organization in the world

ChemistsArcher John Porter Martinand Richard L. M. Synge give the first demonstration of partition chromatography (separation of mixtures) at a meeting of the Biochemical Society held at the National Institute for Medical Research, Hampstead

NASA's Extreme Ultraviolet Explorer (EUVE) launches
Bhaskara 1, Indian Earth resources/meteorology satellite, launched
Russian reconnaissance satellite Cosmos 2344 launches from Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan using a four-stage Proton-K rocket
Auckland named world's most livable city (due to success with COVID) and Damascus named the worst by The Economist Intelligence Unit
Famous Cunard passenger liner Lusitania launches
Mary Pickfordmakes her screen debut at the age of 16

Anglo-Canadian explorerDavid Thompsonreaches the mouth of the Saskatchewan River in Manitoba

Field of the Cloth of Gold; meeting begins between EnglishHenry VIIIand KingFrancis I of Franceat Balinghem, France
Hawaiian Declaration of Rights is signed
